# CSS variables for theming in SuperAdmin.css (2026-03-10 11:11Z)

_**User**_

Read and update `d:\web development\test\src\pages\SuperAdmin.css` to use CSS variables for theming. Replace these hardcoded colors:

Color → Variable mapping:
- `#0f172a` → `var(--bg-page)`
- `#1e293b` → `var(--bg-surface)` or `var(--bg-input)` for input backgrounds  
- `#334155` → `var(--border-default)` for borders, `var(--bg-elevated)` for backgrounds
- `#475569` → `var(--border-subtle)`
- `#64748b` → `var(--text-placeholder)`
- `#94a3b8` → `var(--text-muted)`
- `#cbd5e1` → `var(--text-label)`
- `#e2e8f0` → `var(--text-secondary)`
- `#f1f5f9` → `var(--text-primary)`
- `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4)` for sidebar overlay → `var(--bg-overlay)`
- `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3)` for menu shadow → `var(--shadow-md)` where it's a box-shadow

DO NOT change accent/status colors (blues, greens, reds, ambers, purples). Those stay the same in both themes.
Also do NOT change any rgba() with colored channels (red/green/blue/amber) — only black rgba overlays.

Only replace the slate/neutral palette colors listed above. Use the StrReplace tool for each change.

---

_**Agent (model composer-1.5, mode Agent)**_
